sanskrit:
अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतं च यत्।
असदित्युच्यते पार्थ न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह॥
transliteration:
aśraddhayā hutaṃ dattaṃ tapas taptaṃ kṛtaṃ ca yat |
asad ity ucyate pārtha na ca tat pretya no iha ||
literal_meaning: Whatever sacrifice is offered, gift given, austerity performed, or act done without faith is called 'asat' (non-real), O Pārtha; it is of no account here or hereafter.
contextual_meaning: Actions lose psychological potency when performed without inner conviction; ritual without authentic engagement becomes empty performance, producing neither present satisfaction nor future growth.
psychological_state: Performative burnout—ritualized behavior stripped of personal meaning leading to covert despair.
trigger_scenario: Going through the motions at work, in relationships, or in self-care routines while feeling internally hollow and questioning 'What’s the point?'
anchor_text: Let every deed be lit by the flame of your own belief.
